**Architectural commitments honored:** AC-1 (Tier-1 IEP integration), AC-2 (append-only), AC-11 (spec-version pinning in SkillVersion), AC-12 (tri-linkage).

**Per-repo distinguishability:** label `repo:iec` + Doc + GitHub fields below.

**Children:** IEC-N1 (boundary table), IEC-N2 (ER diagram update). Product epic 0r8m's children (0r8m.1, 0r8m.2, 0r8m.8) coordinate through this RC.

title: Build 'j-rig emit-refiner-pass' CLI command (parallel to existing 'j-rig emit-evidence') — emits skill-refiner-pass/v1 Evidence Bundle rows
description: New CLI command in j-rig-binary-eval/packages/cli/src/commands/emit-refiner-pass.ts. Pattern: parallel to emit-evidence.ts. Usage: j-rig emit-refiner-pass --skill <id> --proposal <hash> --decision <accepted|rejected> [--rekor] [--signing-mode staging|prod]. Reads SkillVersion + EditProposal from .j-rig/refiner/store/; builds predicate body matching skill-refiner-pass/v1 schema; wraps in in-toto Statement v1; optional cosign sign + Rekor upload. Uses audit-harness emit-evidence wrapper for signing. Plan ref: 025 § 4 Phase C j-rig changes.

title: Design skill-refiner-pass/v1 predicate schema (intent-eval-core/schemas/v1/skill-refiner-pass.schema.json) + register in index.json
description: The signable predicate carried by Evidence Bundle rows during optimization. URI: https://evals.intentsolutions.io/skill-optimization-step/v1 (CISO binding: evals not labs). Body: skill_version_id, edit_proposal_hash, score_pre, score_post, decision (accepted|rejected), decision_reason, proposer_model, optimizer_seed, timestamp. Signs into prod Rekor ONLY after bd_000-projects-uprg closes. Plan ref: § 4 Phase C.

title: Implement SkillVersion Zod validator (src/validators/v1/skill-version.ts) via pnpm run codegen:validators + export from index
description: After schema lands (0r8m.1), regenerate Zod validator. Hand-wrap with cross-field invariants. Tests cover valid record, invalid hash, invariant violations. Pattern: mirror existing GateResultV1 validator. Plan ref: § 4 Phase C, § 5 critical files.

title: Design SkillVersion entity schema at intent-eval-core/schemas/v1/skill-version.schema.json (14th canonical entity — additive, joins existing 13)
description: First kernel task on Epic 3. SkillVersion is ADDITIVE to existing 13 entities (does NOT replace SkillSnapshot — complementary). Fields per plan § 4 Phase C: id (uuidv7), skill_id (kebab-slug), skill_doc_hash (sha256), parent_version_id (lineage), edit_proposal_hash, eval_set_hash, score_record, accepted_by, accepted_at, signing_mode, rekor_log_index. Cross-field invariants per bd_000-projects-1is2 (CISO co-author): rekor_log_index non-null iff signing_mode='production'. Plan ref: § 4 Phase C.

title: Ship Skill Refiner evidence + Tier-1 IEP kernel integration — SkillVersion entity (14th canonical) + skill-refiner-pass/v1 predicate URI + signed evidence reports
description:
Epic 3 of the j-rig sub-product roadmap (j-rig brand canon, 2026-05-26).

j-rig Evidence is the report production + signed-attestation sub-product. Tier-1 IEP integration promotes SkillVersion to the 14th canonical entity in intent-eval-core (joining the existing 13). Mints skill-optimization-step/v1 predicate URI on evals.intentsolutions.io.

Architectural commits:
- intent-eval-core: schemas/v1/skill-version.schema.json + skill-optimization-step.schema.json + Zod validators + index registrations + CHANGELOG entry + version bump to 0.3.0
- intent-eval-lab: Blueprint B § 7 normative spec extension + canonical glossary entries
- j-rig: new j-rig emit-skill-step CLI command (parallel to existing emit-evidence)
- intent-rollout-gate: M5 substantive runtime consumes skill-optimization-step/v1 rows as enrichment

j-rig Evidence report pipeline:
- Markdown AAR canonical: <repo>/000-docs/NNN-RL-REPT-jrig-keeper-<skill>-<date>.md (10 required sections per § E.1)
- HTML projection: deterministically rendered from MD; hosted at evals.intentsolutions.io/reports/<skill-id>/<sha-prefix>/ via Hugo template
- Signed Evidence Bundle: in-toto Statement v1 anchored via cosign + sigstore Rekor

Companion P0 dependencies:
- bd_000-projects-uprg — Evidence Bundle predicate compatibility policy (BLOCKS this epic — one-way Rekor door)
- bd_000-projects-9pi3 — OTel semconv pin (BLOCKS kernel v0.3.0 ship)
- bd_000-projects-59tx — Release workflow failure alerting (CROSS-CUTTING)

Plan: intent-eval-lab/000-docs/025-PP-PLAN-j-rig-keeper-pretrip-evidence-2026-05-26.md § 4 Phase C+E.
PR: jeremylongshore/intent-eval-lab#77.
Supersedes deps: closed bead bd_000-projects-6qyw.3 transferred its uprg+9pi3 deps here.
acceptance_criteria: @intentsolutions/core@0.3.0 published with SkillVersion entity + skill-optimization-step/v1 predicate. j-rig emit-skill-step CLI shipped. Blueprint B § 7 extended. ISEDC Session 7 DR ratifying URI namespace filed. jrig-evidence-report v1.0.0-draft SPEC.md committed. HTML renderer live at evals.intentsolutions.io/reports/. One real report generated end-to-end (Epic 2 demo).

title: Add Release workflow failure alerting (ntfy 'prod-alerts' topic) across IEP repos
description:
Per IEP thinker-panel review (Gregg finding #1, 2026-05-25). j-rig Release workflow failed 3 consecutive times since v1.0.0 (runs 26350224382, 26197406480, 26140831171) with NO alert path. Each was caught by ad-hoc gh run list — nobody got paged.

Action per repo (j-rig + intent-eval-core + audit-harness + intent-rollout-gate when M5 lands):
1. Add 'failure:' step to .github/workflows/release.yml that POSTs to ntfy topic 'prod-alerts' (http://intentsolutions:8080).
2. Add scheduled release-health.yml that runs daily and pings on any 'failure' conclusion in latest run.

USE-method 'Errors' axis was pinned >0 with zero monitoring. Release pipeline degraded steady-state. Sweep precondition: j-rig CI fix lands (PR #76 in flight).

title: Pin OTel semantic conventions in @intentsolutions/core BEFORE v0.2.0 ships
description:
Per IEP thinker-panel review (Gregg finding #2 + most-costly-to-recover-from, 2026-05-25). Blueprint B § 4.3 specifies OTel events (runtime.dedup, bundle.emission.refused, replay.verdict, etc.) but the ATTRIBUTE NAMES are not pinned. Five emitters (j-rig, audit-harness, intent-rollout-gate, intent-eval-lab tests, intent-eval-core itself) will drift across naming (eval_run_id vs evalRunId vs run.id).

Action: add schemas/v1/otel-attributes.yaml to intent-eval-core (OTel semconv format), export named constants from @intentsolutions/core/otel/v1, add normative 'must emit' attribute list per predicate kind to Blueprint B § 7.2.

Child of iel-E12 (Canonical runtime event taxonomy). Without this, iel-E12's event-name pinning is necessary but not sufficient. Must land before v0.2.0 ships — recovery cost after consumers emit drifted-attribute spans rises super-linearly with retained-trace count.

title: Write Evidence Bundle predicate compatibility policy BEFORE first prod-Rekor anchor
description:
Per IEP thinker-panel review (Kleppmann finding #4, Fowler most-costly-to-recover-from, 2026-05-25). Rekor anchoring is a one-way door — once gate-result/v1 rows are signed into prod Rekor, the URI namespace is permanent forever. The compatibility policy MUST be written and ratified BEFORE iah-E06 pre-flight passes.

Policy MUST specify: (a) FORWARD rule (v1 consumer ignores unknown fields in v2 rows), (b) BACKWARD rule (v2 consumer reads v1 rows unmodified), (c) MIXING rule (bundles MAY contain rows of different versions; consumers process each row independently), (d) DEPRECATION rule (vN supported for at least 3 years after v(N+1)).

Landing place: intent-eval-lab/specs/evidence-bundle/COMPATIBILITY.md. Should be made an iec-E12 precondition.

This is a one-way door. Recovery cost = abandon v1 namespace and start over at v2 (forfeits first-mover-on-attested-evidence claim).

title: iec-E12b — Audit-harness iah-E04 second-emitter shape sketch (paper)
description: Per DR 018 § 6.4 CSO + CFO joint binding precondition. Sketch on paper the EvidenceBundlePayload shape audit-harness's iah-E04 emit-evidence subcommand will need. Output: design doc showing how audit-harness will populate the kernel-canonical payload shape. Blocks iec-E12 final PR open per binding precondition: if no second-emitter shape can be articulated, the kernel PR is HELD. Parent: bd_000-projects-wjh6; blocks iec-E12.

title: iec-E12a — Cross-field invariant enumeration (CISO co-author)
description: Per DR 018 § 6.4. CISO co-authors enumeration of cross-field invariants from Blueprint B § 7.3 line 792 BEFORE iec-E12 kernel PR opens. Output: design-doc or PR-body section enumerating each invariant + Zod .refine() implementation pattern. Once these invariants ship in kernel, they are effectively immutable. Parent: bd_000-projects-wjh6; blocks iec-E12.

title: iec-E12 — Kernel @intentsolutions/core v0.2.0: EvidenceBundlePayload + cross-field invariants
description: Per ISEDC Session 5 Q2 decision (DR 018). Ship kernel v0.2.0 (minor bump, additive — no breaking changes to existing kernel consumers). Adds: (1) EvidenceBundlePayload type + Zod schema (the JSON-array wire format); (2) folded EvidenceStatement row shape from j-rig (in-toto Statement v1 carrying gate-result/v1 predicate); (3) cross-field invariants from Blueprint B § 7.3 line 792 enforced as Zod .refine() calls — subject[0].name === predicate.gate_id AND subject[0].digest.sha256 (no prefix) === predicate.input_hash (no sha256: prefix); (4) extensions: Record<string, unknown> escape hatch for experimental fields. Binding preconditions: PR drafted against ≥2 prospective emitters before merge; CISO co-authors invariant enumeration before PR opens. Parent: bd_000-projects-wjh6 (ISEDC Session 5).

title: iep-kernel-shadow-inventory — Read-only inventory of 11 canonical types across 5 repos
description: Read-only audit. Walk audit-harness, j-rig-binary-eval, intent-rollout-gate, intent-eval-lab, and intent-eval-core source trees. Identify every local definition of the 11 canonical kernel types: EvidenceBundle, EvalSpec, EvalRun, EvalCase, SessionTrace, Provider, JudgeAdapter, ScoringConfig, MatcherInputPattern, AssertionExpression, GateResultV1. Produce intent-eval-lab/000-docs/016-RR-LAND-kernel-shadow-inventory-2026-05-20.md with per-repo per-type breakdown, file paths, line numbers, retention sites (test fixtures), and migration target list. Unblocks Priority 1 parallel migration work.
